name: Smoked Hamburger Salami - Three Versions
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------------SALAMI #2---------------------------------
5 lb Hamburger
5 tsp Curing Salt
4 tbsp Wine, white, dry
1 1/2 ts Garlic powder
2 1/2 tbsp Chili powder
1 1/4 tsp Cumin, ground
2 tbsp Sugar, brown
---------------------------------SALAMI #3---------------------------------
5 lb Hamburger
5 tsp Curing Salt
3 tbsp Wine, dry, red
1 1/4 tsp Garlic powder
2 1/4 tbsp Mustard seed, whole
1 1/2 tbsp Basil, ground
1 1/2 tbsp Oregano, ground
3/4 cup Parmesan cheese
2 tbsp Sugar, brown
Mix all ingredients thoroughly then cover and chill for at least 24 hours,
or longer. Divide into four portions. Roll each portion into a roll that is
2 1/3"-3 inches in diameter and wrap with inexpensive large hole nylon netting.
Tie the ends securely with string (netting may be omitted, but rolls will flatten
out during smoking).
Smoke in smoker for 8-12 hours using your choice of fuel. This assumes a cold smoke.
Adjust times accordingly, but ensure salami is fully cured throughout.
Remove from smoker and remove netting. Dry thoroughly with paper towel. Wrap
in foil and refrigerate up to 3 weeks or freeze up to 6 months. It is so easy
to make that you don't have to make large quantities and store.
Curing Salt (VERY IMPORTANT)
Use only 'Tender Quick' or prepared curing salt. Regular salt does not contain the
necessary flavors, taste, or nitrate for a quick cure and will not bind the meat.
This is essential for successful recipes. You can purchase prepared curing salts
from companies like Mortons, Lowery's, and others, or check your local supermarket,
butcher shop, wine store, or feed store.
